summary: Round-2 remediation (commit bf10d40) closes the two major litmus-test gaps from round 1 (warmup/migration invocation + sidecar cleanup) with genuinely observable assertions against recording fakes and real filesystem state. The minor gaps from round 1 (corrupt-name regex, sweep failure path, mainActivityFqn) are also resolved. New tests are well-written: each has a clear litmus-test failure mode (delete production line → counter stays 0 or file survives → assertion fails). Deferred minors #5/#6/#7 remain as agreed. No new regressions introduced; one nit on the crash-process negative test using a fixed 100ms sleep, and one optional enrichment for the sweep-failure assertion message, both captured as minor.

findings.issue: Round-1 finding #1 negative branch (`testHousekeepingSkippedInCrashProcess`) uses a fixed `Thread.sleep(100)` before asserting counters are 0. This is a rather soft negative assertion: if a future change wraps the `packageName` guard incorrectly and schedules warmup/migration on a slow dispatcher (e.g. network-backed), a 100ms window could theoretically race and produce a false pass. The risk is low because `appScope.launch` on `Dispatchers.Default` completes well inside 100ms on a test JVM, and `warmupCoordinator.warmupDefault()` is now synchronous so it would increment synchronously. Still worth tightening since the positive-path test already demonstrates the counters can land within ~20ms.
findings.recommendation: Keep the 100ms settle but harden the negative path by also running a small active-work barrier: after the sleep, synchronously invoke a no-op coroutine on the same `recordingScope` (e.g. `runBlocking(recordingScope.coroutineContext) { yield() }`) to drain anything the guard leaked. Or — simpler — extend the settle window to 300ms and add a comment citing the max observed positive-path latency so future readers understand the safety margin. Not blocking: the existing assertion still catches the obvious regression (removing the guard entirely).

findings.issue: `awaitHistoryWriteEntry()` — used only by the new round-2 test `sweepZombieChatsFailureLogsHistoryWriteAndDoesNotThrow` — silently returns after the 2s deadline without throwing. If the log entry never lands, the subsequent `assertTrue` on `errorLogFile.readText()` produces a less informative failure (`expected history-write entry for sweep failure; got: <empty>`) than the sibling `awaitAttachmentSaveEntry` helpers, which at least force a file read. Inconsistent with `SanctumApplicationTest.awaitCounter` which throws `AssertionError` on timeout with a diagnostic message.
findings.recommendation: Align the helper with `awaitCounter` — on timeout throw `AssertionError("expected history-write entry within 2000ms; errorLog contents: ${errorLogFile.takeIf { it.exists() }?.readText() ?: "<missing>"}")`. That way a regression that logs to the wrong component (say, `attachment-save`) shows the actual log content in the failure message instead of an empty-file assertion. Cosmetic, not blocking.

resolvedFindings.verification: `testHousekeepingRunsInMainProcess` installs `RecordingWarmupCoordinator` / `RecordingSettingsMigrationHelper` (both subclassing production now that `open` qualifiers were added) and polls three counters with 2s deadline / 20ms sleep. Poll cadence is appropriate: `warmupCoordinator.warmupDefault()` is now called synchronously inline (no outer `launch`), so `warmupCalls` is incremented before `onCreate()` returns — poll returns on first check. `settingsMigrationHelper.migrateIfNeeded()` runs on `appScope.launch` Dispatchers.Default; `startupHousekeeper.run()` on Dispatchers.IO — 2s is ample. Negative `testHousekeepingSkippedInCrashProcess` now asserts all three counters stay at 0 in the `:crash` process. Litmus test: deleting `warmupCoordinator.warmupDefault()` from SanctumApplication.kt:38 flips the positive test red (counter stays 0). Deleting `appScope.launch { settingsMigrationHelper.migrateIfNeeded() }` flips it red the same way.

Test now pre-plants `sanctum.db-journal` (0x11), `sanctum.db-wal` (0x22), `sanctum.db-shm` (0x33) before invoking `provideSanctumDatabase` and asserts each `.exists()` is false after recovery. Litmus test: deleting any of the three `.delete()` calls at AppModule.kt:77-79 (or lines 86-88 in the rename-refused branch) causes the corresponding sidecar to survive and flips `assertFalse` red. Assertion messages explicitly call out the zombie-state risk for grep-ability.

`Regex("^${Regex.escape(SanctumDatabase.DATABASE_NAME)}\\.corrupt_\\d{8}-\\d{6}$")` matches the recommended shape exactly. Regression to `Locale.getDefault()` in a locale that renders non-ASCII digits (e.g. `ar-EG`) would break `\d{8}` matching and fail the test. Comment at line 82 documents Locale.ROOT intent.

`sweepZombieChatsFailureLogsHistoryWriteAndDoesNotThrow` injects a `FakeChatRepository` whose `sweepZombieChats` throws `IOException("synthetic sweep failure")`, then asserts the log contains `ERROR [history-write]` AND `sweepZombieChats failed`. Matches the production change in StartupHousekeeper.kt:15 (`SWEEP_LOG_COMPONENT = "history-write"`). Test comment at line 135 explicitly notes the implicit no-throw assertion. Litmus test: removing the try/catch at StartupHousekeeper.kt:56-65 would cause `run()` to propagate the IOException, which `runBlocking { housekeeper.run() }` would rethrow — test fails with the IOException, not the assertion. Also covers the component-name change: reverting to `LOG_COMPONENT` (`attachment-save`) would flip `contains("ERROR [history-write]")` red.

Not addressed; explicitly deferred by the team. Duplication remains (SanctumApplicationTest has `NullChatRepository` as 7-method no-op stub; StartupHousekeeperTest has `FakeChatRepository` with counter + throw hook — slightly different surfaces, so the shared-fixture fix would need to expose both variants). Reasonable deferral; no change of opinion.

Not addressed. The round-1 fix actually expanded this pattern (WarmupCoordinator and SettingsMigrationHelper both became `open class` with `open fun` methods so recording fakes can subclass). Production-surface leak is now slightly larger (two additional open classes), but still behaviourally a double and not a correctness issue. Interface-seam refactor remains a follow-up worth tracking but not blocking.

Not addressed; deferred. Note: round-1 fix added `@Volatile` to `corruptionOccurred` — the existing trivial getter/setter tests do not exercise the volatile read/write barrier (would require a multi-thread happens-before test, which Robolectric cannot deterministically produce). Low priority.

`testMainActivityFqnAssignedEvenInCrashProcess` sets `mainActivityFqn = null` in `@Before`, writes `${packageName}:crash` as the bound process name, invokes `onCreate()`, and asserts the field equals `"app.sanctum.machina.MainActivity"`. `@After` restores the previous value to avoid JVM-global pollution. Litmus test: moving the assignment at SanctumApplication.kt:46 inside the `packageName` guard block flips the test red (field stays null). Assertion message explicitly cites the DownloadWorker background-process requirement.
